chunk_summary: This workflow outlines a comprehensive outpatient approach for adolescent nutrition visits, addressing growth, menstrual health, athletic training, and disordered eating, with screening, triage, and phenotype-specific management strategies.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of using a unified outpatient workflow for adolescent nutrition visits.
Learn to screen for growth, menstrual health, and psychosocial factors, including eating disorders and athletic training loads.
Identify criteria for urgent referral or admission based on medical instability.
Apply phenotype-specific management pathways for stable patients.
key_facts: Screening includes height, weight, BMI plotting, pubertal stage, 24-hour dietary recall, and menstrual cycle assessment.
Urgent referral is required for bradycardia (HR <50/min daytime or <45/min night), hypotension (BP <90/45 mm Hg), or temperature <35.6°C.
Menstrual cycle is treated as a vital sign, with attention to flooding, clots, dysmenorrhea, and school absenteeism.
Psychosocial screening includes HEADSS and SCOFF for eating disorders when indicated.
Phenotype-specific pathways include growth spurt support, menstrual symptom management, athlete/RED-S care, and eating disorder treatment.
reference_quote: Treat the menstrual cycle as a vital sign (ACOG, 'Menstruation in girls and adolescents: Using the menstrual cycle as a vital sign').
document_key_topics: Menstrual health as a vital sign
Management of eating disorders and RED-S
